## Problem

The promotions section served by `sv-playbook serve` renders mojibake: `content/ui/index.html` contains 10 double-encoded UTF-8 sequences (`Ã¡`, `Ã³`, `Ãš`) introduced when the promotion projection UI was authored. The console shows "Estado mecÃ¡nico", "integraciÃ³n", "Ãšltimo cambio" instead of "mecánico", "integración", "Último cambio". No gate inspects static UI asset encoding, so corrupted text can reach the human surface silently (HJ-015: UI is a product surface; ENTRY-004: no claim without evidence).

## Task

1. Add `src/serve/ui-static-assets.test.ts` with a test named exactly `static UI assets contain no double-encoded UTF-8 sequences` that scans every `content/ui/**/*.{html,js,css}` file and fails when it finds the double-encoding signature (`Ã` followed by a second mojibake byte pattern, i.e. U+00C3 followed by a character in the Latin-1 Supplement range produced by re-encoding UTF-8 as Latin-1). The test must pass on clean assets and a companion case must prove the detector fails on a corrupted fixture string.
2. Fix the 10 sequences in `content/ui/index.html`: `Ã¡`→`á`, `Ã³`→`ó`, `Ãš`→`Ú`. No other content changes.
3. Record the incident in `docs/backlog.md` as a graduated entry pointing to this packet.

## RED test

- Test: `static UI assets contain no double-encoded UTF-8 sequences` in `src/serve/ui-static-assets.test.ts`.
- Expected failure cause: the test name itself (node:test prints the failing test name); it fails today because `content/ui/index.html` contains the sequences.

## Reuse pointers

- `src/serve/server.ts` serves these assets from `content/ui` (`UI_ROOT`).
- `src/check/source-tree.ts` shows how the project walks source trees for mechanical checks.
